strengths: Distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second, demonstrating backend scale exposure.
Current role at re:cinq includes MCP tooling and AI-agent infrastructure contributions, showing some agentic-workflow familiarity.
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, Swift, C/C++) and decade-spanning exposure across web, mobile, embedded, and cloud contexts.
concerns: Zero engineering leadership track record: every role on the resume carries an individual-contributor title (Software Developer, Senior Developer, Staff Engineer); no team management, hiring, org scaling, or leadership layer development is evidenced anywhere, directly disqualifying against the hard requirement for 5+ years of senior technical leadership scaling teams from 10 to 30+ people.

Agentic-AI experience is shallow and brand-new: the only agentic work cited is MCP code generators at re:cinq starting April 2025 (~3 months tenure); no production multi-agent systems, no LLM evaluation pipelines, no LangGraph or equivalent orchestration, and no demonstrated operation of AI systems under financial or regulatory stakes.

No fintech-infrastructure depth: the RIDE Capital role was a developer-level engagement practicing TDD on investment tooling, not ownership of credit systems, payment flows, working-capital models, or compliance infrastructure across jurisdictions — all of which are core to this CTO mandate.

key_factors.factor: Agentic AI experience nascent and unproven at production scale
Absent fintech domain and regulatory expertise

response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a technically broad individual contributor with no demonstrated transition into engineering leadership at any level; the resume contains no evidence of direct reports, org design, hiring decisions, or cross-team governance — the foundational requirement for this executive role. The agentic-AI exposure is limited to a single three-month stint building developer tooling, far removed from the production multi-agent, real-time inference, and LLM evaluation infrastructure the JD demands. Compounding this, the candidate's fintech contact is surface-level developer work with no ownership of credit risk, payment flows, regulatory compliance, or working-capital systems, leaving all three pillars of the job description — executive leadership, production agentic AI, and fintech infrastructure — substantially unmet.
